title: centaurea pullata subsp. pullata
intro: Centaurea pullata subsp. pullata, also known as the Alpine knapweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and deeply divided leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea pullata subsp. pullata include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
